<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Sentinel Horn](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2678)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Sky King's Tomb</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Auditory" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=16" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #195: Heavy is the Crown](/Sources.aspx?ID=210) pg. 80

**Price** 250 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This curved brass horn is a virtuoso instrument with a surprisingly deep tone, etched with images of interlocking lines and swirls. _Sentinel horns_ come in pairs, and each works with only the other of its pair. The listed Price is for a pair of _sentinel horns_.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;  **Frequency** once per round;   **Effect** You play a specific tune on the horn. When you do, the other horn also plays the same tune, not matter how far away it is, as long as it is on the same plane.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Sentinel Horn

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 8"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Sigil of the First Clan](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2679)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Sky King's Tomb</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #195: Heavy is the Crown](/Sources.aspx?ID=210) pg. 80

**Price** 430 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn necklace

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This intricate gold and adamantine clasp depicts the sigil of Clan Taargick, house of the famed King Taargick, founder of Tar Taargadth. When displayed prominently, other dwarves give more weight to your words. You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) and [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) checks against dwarves and against anyone who considers themselves a staunch ally of Clan Taargick. Against anyone who considers Clan Taargick an enemy, such as worshippers of [Droskar](/Deities.aspx?ID=28) and many [orcs](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=19), you instead take a –1 circumstance penalty to Diplomacy and Intimidation checks.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> command;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You wield the authority of Clan Taargick like a gavel, enforcing your word as law. You cast 1st-level [_command_](/Spells.aspx?ID=45) with a DC of 24.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Sigil of the First Clan

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 10"
    pfs=""
>
[Tome of Dripping Shadows](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2680)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Sky King's Tomb</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Grimoire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=387" />
<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=92" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Shadow" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=143" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #195: Heavy is the Crown](/Sources.aspx?ID=210) pg. 81

**Price** 1,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This book constantly drips evanescent tendrils of shadow that seethe and writhe. The tendrils have a will of their own, retreating from light sources and curling toward darkness. While you hold the tome, you gain [darkvision](/Rules.aspx?ID=415); if you already have darkvision, you gain [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) instead.

 Preparing spells from this tome strengthens your shadow magic and gives you a greater understanding of shadow spells you add to this tome. Any [shadow](/Traits.aspx?ID=143) spells you prepare from this tome that are at least 2 levels lower than the highest-level spell you can cast are treated as 1 level higher for the purposes of [counteracting](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) them. When you add a shadow spell to this tome, you can prepare heightened versions of that spell up to the highest level of that spell that you are able to cast.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** A target of a shadow spell you prepared from the grimoire critically fails a saving throw against the spell;  **Effect** You recall a fragment of the shadow magic that suffuses your target and wrap it around yourself like a caul. The triggering creature's saving throw result is a failure, not a critical failure. For the next minute, you become [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) to all creatures and you are [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=22) from the triggering creature, regardless of what precise sense it has. If you use a [hostile action](/Rules.aspx?ID=300) against the triggering creature, you become only concealed to it (rather than hidden) at the end of the hostile action.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Tome of Dripping Shadows

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 14"
    pfs=""
>
[Cavern's Heart](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2681)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Sky King's Tomb</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Earth" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=55" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #195: Heavy is the Crown](/Sources.aspx?ID=210) pg. 81

**Price** 4,100 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** etched onto medium or heavy armor

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Usage etched onto medium or heavy armor This armor can channel your life force into an enhanced [_wall of stone_](/Spells.aspx?ID=365).

**Activate** <actions string="Three Actions" /> command;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** By stamping your foot on the ground and calling out to spirits of stone, you summon a stone wall. This is a _wall of stone_, except that the wall is tied to your own life force. When the stone wall is damaged, you are damaged instead. If you are reduced to 0 HP, the wall is destroyed. The effect is dismissed if you move more than 30 feet away from the stone wall or if you spend a single action with the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) trait.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Cavern's Heart

markdown:
May contain spoilers from Season of Ghosts **Source** [Pathfinder #196: The Summer That Never Was](/Sources.aspx?ID=214) pg. 81 **Price** 100 gp **Usage** worn cloak **Bulk** L --- While [Yaezhing](/Deities.aspx?ID=184) is feared and seldom worshipped in the open, some regions of Tian Xia see him as a god of necessary evil and their only hope for justice. This garment is often worn by bounty hunters or priests of Yaezhing, yet non-worshippers of the god of harsh justice sometimes wear this item without fear of religious persecution. The red cape appears almost black while in the shadows, with a lighter red mandala pattern on it that resembles a shuriken. When you invest a _cape of justice_, you must designate a single creature as your offender. You must have met the creature before, or the creature should have a persona well known to the public (for instance, a bandit's epithet, if not their face as shown on a wanted poster). You gain a +1 item bonus to [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=53) this creature. If you don't designate a creature as your offender when you invest the _cape of justice_, or if 24 hours pass without you either slaying or capturing your offender, your thoughts become worried and distracted and you take a –1 item penalty to Perception checks for the next 24 hours. **Activate** command, Interact ([force](/Traits.aspx?ID=75)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Effect** By using a free hand to grab the edge of your cape and give it a quick flourish, you cause a red, shuriken-shaped bolt of force to fire at a target within 60 feet that you can see. The shuriken hits automatically and deals 1d4+1 force damage, or 2d4+2 force damage if the target is your designated offender. If the target is within 30 feet, you can attempt to Demoralize the target as a free action after the shuriken strikes them. **Activate** command, Interact ([incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You produce [manacles](/Equipment.aspx?ID=32) from the cape and then fling them at a Medium or Small bipedal target within 30 feet. The target must attempt a DC 18 Reflex save. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected, and the manacles vanish.
**Success** The manacles strike the target's legs but fail to latch properly. The target is [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) until the start of your next turn, at which point the manacles vanish.
**Failure** The manacles lock on the target's legs, causing the target to take a –15-foot circumstance penalty to its Speeds for 1 minute, or until it [Escapes](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) or is freed by someone using [Pick a Lock](/Actions.aspx?ID=70) (DC 18), after which the manacles vanish.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the target is [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24) for as long as the manacles remain in place.
name: Cape of Justice

May contain spoilers from Season of Ghosts **Source** [Pathfinder #196: The Summer That Never Was](/Sources.aspx?ID=214) pg. 81 **Price** 100 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L **Base Weapon** [Dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=3) --- The blade of this _+1 [low-grade silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=379) [dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=3)_ has a sickly red tinge. Though once used to send souls to empower Kugaptee, the fury of those slain by the blade now allow its wielder to periodically strike back against [fiends](/Traits.aspx?ID=70). **Activate** command ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You score a critical hit against a fiend; **Effect** Vengeful echoes of the fallen lance out into the psyche of the fiend struck. After the normal effects of the critical hit, the target must roll a DC 18 Fortitude save. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature becomes distracted by vengeful spirits and becomes [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) until the start of your next turn.
**Failure** As success, but the creature is also [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) for 1 round.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the creature is slowed 1 for 1 minute.
name: Fiend's Hunger

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs=""
>
[Hongrui's Gratitude](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2684)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Season of Ghosts</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #196: The Summer That Never Was](/Sources.aspx?ID=214) pg. 82

**Price** 60 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn cloak

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Given as thanks for honoring the memories of three unfortunate travelers who met a tragic end in the Willowshore Hinterlands, this red-hooded cloak is decorated with an image of an elaborately dressed performer whose two companions hold a lantern and umbrella for her.

**Activate** <actions string="Three Actions" /> command, envision, Interact ([evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=100)) **Frequency** once per day;  **Requirements** You don't have the umbrella activation of this cloak active; **Effect** With a wave of the cloak to the left, you cause the image of the lantern-holding companion on the cloak to vanish. A floating red lantern appears at your side and follows you at an arm's length while casting [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=407) in a 20-foot radius (and [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=408) for the next 20 feet) like a [torch](/Equipment.aspx?ID=60). For 1 hour, while the light from this lantern is active, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) checks. You can Activate the cloak again to dismiss the torch.

**Activate** <actions string="Three Actions" /> command, envision, Interact ([abjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=2)) **Frequency** once per day;  **Requirements** You don't have the lantern activation of this cloak active; **Effect** With a wave of the cloak to the right, you cause the image of the umbrella-holding companion on the cloak to vanish. An indestructible red wax paper umbrella appears above you and follows you, shielding you from weather effects, such as rain or bright sunlight. For 1 hour, while this umbrella is active, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) checks. You can Activate the cloak again to dismiss the umbrella.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Hongrui's Gratitude

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs=""
>
[Matsuki's Medicinal Wine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2685)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Season of Ghosts</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Drug" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=261" />
<trait label="Ingested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Poison" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=126" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #196: The Summer That Never Was](/Sources.aspx?ID=214) pg. 82

**Price** 12 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Old Matsuki's private brew has quite the kick. In addition to making you tipsy, this medicinal wine works wonders in helping to recover from disease—including (ironically) drug addiction. Matsuki's medicinal wine functions as [alcohol](/Equipment.aspx?ID=622), except that its saving throw is a DC 18 Fortitude save. In addition, upon drinking a dose of Matsuki's medicinal wine, you gain a +2 item bonus to Fortitude saving throws against [diseases](/Traits.aspx?ID=46) and [poisons](/Traits.aspx?ID=126) for 24 hours. This applies to your daily save against a disease's progression; however, the bonus doesn't apply to any checks you attempt against Matsuki's medical wine itself.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Matsuki's Medicinal Wine

>
[Noppera-Bo Hood](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2686)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Season of Ghosts</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=92" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #196: The Summer That Never Was](/Sources.aspx?ID=214) pg. 82

**Price** 85 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn helmet

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This unpleasant-looking hood appears to be a completely smooth, round sack of skin that feels uncannily warm to the touch. When you wear this hood and invest it, it merges with your head and face, becoming imperceptible as a worn item except on close examination, which reveals a slight oily sheen to your facial features. You can cause minor shifts and changes to your features while wearing a _noppera-bo hood_; this counts as having a [disguise kit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=19) to [Impersonate](/Actions.aspx?ID=46) any creature that is the same ancestry as you.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> envision ([transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You focus on the hood's magic, and then gain the effects of 1st-level [_illusory disguise_](/Spells.aspx?ID=159), though it's a [transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157) effect rather than an [illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=92).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Noppera-Bo Hood

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #196: The Summer That Never Was](/Sources.aspx?ID=214) pg. 82

**Price** 60 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** tattooed on the body

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The _spider lily tattoo_ marks you as a trusted member of Granny Hu's network. This crimson tattoo fades and becomes invisible within a day of being applied, reappearing only when you Activate it, when you gain the [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=9) condition, or when you die. The higher the doomed value, the more vivid the color. If your tattoo is plainly visible, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) checks against all creatures that can see the tattoo, but you take a –1 item penalty to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) checks to [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=50) on those who understand the actual meaning of the _spider lily tattoo_ (including all of Willowshore's citizens).

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> envision ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32))   **Requirements** Your tattoo isn't currently visible; **Effect** You make the tattoo visible. You can use this activation again to make the tattoo not visible, unless you have the doomed condition.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> envision ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** An [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) creature detects you for the first time;  **Effect** The _spider lily tattoo_ manipulates your life force to make you appear to be undead for a short time. Attempt a [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) check against the triggering undead creature's Perception DC. On a success, the triggering undead believes you're undead as well—a [mindless](/Traits.aspx?ID=108) undead is likely to ignore you, while a sapient undead might react with curiosity or confusion. You can continue attempting Deception checks each round as a single action to Sustain the effect for up to 1 minute.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Spider Lily Tattoo

May contain spoilers from Season of Ghosts **Source** [Pathfinder #196: The Summer That Never Was](/Sources.aspx?ID=214) pg. 83 **Price** 5 gp --- A _spirit trap_ consists of a net dipped in water that has been steeped with sacred herbs to better combat phantoms. This snare's components function as a [net](/Equipment.aspx?ID=681) when not set up. You set this snare up in a 10-foot-by-10-foot area. The first creature with the [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737) trait that steps into the area must attempt a DC 16 Fortitude save. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected
**Success** The creature is [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) for 1 round.
**Failure** The creature is [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24) for 1 round.
**Critical Failure** The creature is immobilized until it [Escapes](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) (DC 16).
name: Spirit Trap

</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Season of Ghosts</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Price** 60 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Spiked Gauntlet](/Weapons.aspx?ID=11)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This spiky, stony fragment shed from Ugly Cute's carapace fits quite comfortably over the hand. Though a little bulkier than the typical gauntlet, it still functions as a _+1 [spiked gauntlet](/Weapons.aspx?ID=11)_.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You draw upon Ugly Cute's latent spiritual energy to infuse the gauntlet with forceful power. For 1 minute, Ugly Cute's gift gains the advantages of a [ghost touch](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297) property rune and deals an additional 2 force damage on a successful Strike.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Ugly Cute's Gift

>
[Animal-Turning Fulu](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2690)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Season of Ghosts</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Abjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=2" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Fulu" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=378" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Talisman" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=155" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #196: The Summer That Never Was](/Sources.aspx?ID=214) pg. 83

**Price** 4 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to armor

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Reaction" /><row>envision</row></row>

 

**Trigger** A creature with the [animal](/Traits.aspx?ID=9) trait successfully Strikes you.

 
</row>

</column>

---

Frightened animals depicted on this fulu flee in all directions from a central figure (traditionally represented by a human hunter, but sometimes depicted as a skeletal [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) creature or even a [fiend](/Traits.aspx?ID=70) with long, broken arms). You activate this fulu, gaining a +2 item bonus to AC against the triggering Strike. If this causes the Strike to miss, you become [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) from the triggering creature until the start of your next turn.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Animal-Turning Fulu

[Privacy Ward Fulu](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2692)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Season of Ghosts</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Abjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=2" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Fulu" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=378" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #196: The Summer That Never Was](/Sources.aspx?ID=214) pg. 83

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to wall

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This fulu seeks to keep thieves, spies, and unwanted attention away from a room. A depiction of a lock appears in the center of this fulu, which is in turn surrounded by circles of broken keys. When applied to a wall inside a room, all creatures within the room gain an item bonus to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) checks against creatures outside the room.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

>
Privacy Ward Fulu (Hallway)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Price** 4 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The _privacy ward fulu_ can shield a room of up to 10 square feet, grants a +1 item bonus, and has a duration of 1 hour.

pfs="Standard"

The fulu can shield a room of up to 30 square feet, grants a +1 item bonus, and has a duration of 8 hours. If you become [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=22) within the room, you automatically become [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=39) to creatures of the fulu's level or lower.

The fulu can shield a room of up to 90 square feet, grants a +2 item bonus, and has a duration of 24 hours. If you become hidden within the room, you automatically become undetected to creatures of the fulu's level or lower.

A dose of this slightly fizzy, pale-pink tonic vitalizes the body and bolsters the spirit, bringing those suffering from aches and pains brought on by illness some temporary respite. For 8 hours after drinking this elixir, reduce the value of one of the following conditions you're suffering by 1: [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10), [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13), or [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34). A condition whose value is reduced to 0 is not removed; after 8 hours, the condition returns to its original value, unless its duration expired or it was removed by another means.

markdown:
May contain spoilers from Rusthenge **Source** [Rusthenge](/Sources.aspx?ID=215) pg. 60 **Usage** held in 2 hands **Bulk** 2 --- This rusted, coiled iron horn is sizable, appearing similar in shape to a ram's horn but measuring nearly 2 feet in diameter. Flakes of rust constantly fall from this item, and it leaves hands with a rusty red stain that lingers. While the _Horn of Rust's_ power has diminished over the ages since Xar-Azmak's death, those who carry it are still subject to its palpable chaotic influences. If you aren't chaotic evil, you are [enfeebled 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13) while carrying or using the _Horn of Rust_, and if you're lawful or good, you are instead [enfeebled 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13) and stupefied 1. The longer a non-worshipper of Xar-Azmak carries the _Horn of Rust_, the greater the chances a [vloriak](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2727) or other [demon](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=28) (or group of demons) will seek them out to slay them and reclaim the artifact. The frequency of these attacks is left to the GM to determine, but they should occur no less frequently than once a month. These attacks should always be at least moderate encounters; if the PCs don't take them seriously, the attacks could eventually escalate into severe encounters. Some of the _Horn of Rust's_ activated powers consume charges, similar to [how a magic staff functions](/Rules.aspx?ID=748). When its charges are reduced to zero, its activations that require charges can still be used normally. It can be prepared with charges in the same method as a staff, and it counts as a staff for the limit of how many staves you can prepare in a day. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You expend a number of charges from the Horn of Rust to cast a spell from the following list.
  • **Cantrip** [_divine lance_](/Spells.aspx?ID=84) (chaotic or evil damage only)
  • **1st** [_noxious vapors_](/Spells.aspx?ID=704)
  • **2nd** [_shatter_](/Spells.aspx?ID=279)
  • **3rd** [_stinking cloud_](/Spells.aspx?ID=309)
---
The following activations do not consume charges from the Horn of Rust, and they can be utilized even when the artifact has no charges available. **Activate** envision, Interact; **Frequency** once per hour; **Effect** You blow on the horn to unleash a swirling aura of jagged, rusty metal shards that orbit you in a 5-foot emanation for 1 minute. The shards grant you a +1 item bonus to AC. Creatures and objects that begin their turn in this area take 6d6 slashing damage from the shards (DC 19 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). A creature damaged by this aura is exposed to a potent form of [tetanus](/Diseases.aspx?ID=3) that has a level equal to the _Horn of Rust's_ level and has a DC 19 Fortitude save. Worshippers of Xar-Azmak are immune to this effect. **Activate** envision, Interact; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You place the horn's bell against a diseased creature and then inhale into the mouthpiece rather than blow into it. The horn attempts to draw out the disease, casting a [_remove disease_](/Spells.aspx?ID=251) spell to your specification with a [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) modifier of +11. If the _Horn of Rust_ succeeds at countering a disease, it gains a number of charges equal to half the disease's level (rounded down, minimum 1). If it critically fails, not only is the disease not countered, but you are exposed to the disease and must attempt a saving throw against it to resist contracting the affliction. --- **Xar-Azmak Resurrected** If the demon lord Xar-Azmak is resurrected, the _Horn of Rust_ becomes a 20th-level item. Its swirling aura of rusty metal shards grants a +3 item bonus to AC, deals 20d6 slashing damage (DC 43 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save), and the tetanus has a DC 43 Fortitude save. It has a +31 counteract modifier against diseases. You can cast the following additional spells using the artifact's charges.
  • **4th** [_rusting grasp_](/Spells.aspx?ID=711)
  • **5th** [_cloudkill_](/Spells.aspx?ID=42)
  • **6th** [_disintegrate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=76)
  • **7th** [_divine decree_](/Spells.aspx?ID=82) (chaotic or evil only)
  • **8th** [_horrid wilting_](/Spells.aspx?ID=152)
  • **9th** [_disjunction_](/Spells.aspx?ID=77)
  • **10th** [_cataclysm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=32)
**Destruction** The _Horn of Rust_ must be washed with the juice of a fresh peach plucked from an immortal [arboreal](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=8), then dipped into a pool of molten [mithral](/Equipment.aspx?ID=275), and finally cooled with the tears of a remorseful demon. Alternatively, GMs can devise a series of their own quests for the PCs and send them to destroy this ancient artifact. Once the horn is destroyed, Xar-Azmak can never be resurrected.
name: Horn of Rust

---

A brass ear is a short, flared tube with one end narrow enough to comfortably fit against the ear canal. When using it to listen through a door, window, thin wall, or similar barrier, if the barrier would normally increase the DC of your Perception check to hear sounds on the other side, the DC increases by only half as much as normal. It's not suitable for improving your hearing in general, a role better served by a [hearing aids](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1347).

    right="Item 0"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Caltrops](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2708)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 287

**Price** 3 sp

<row gap="medium">

**Hands** 1

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These four-pronged metal spikes can damage a creature's feet. You can scatter caltrops in an empty square adjacent to you with an Interact action. The first creature that moves into that square must succeed at a DC 14 [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) check or take 1d4 piercing damage and 1 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29). A creature taking persistent bleed damage from caltrops takes a –5-foot penalty to its Speed. Spending an Interact action to pluck the caltrops free reduces the DC to stop the bleeding. Once a creature takes damage from caltrops, enough are ruined that other creatures moving into the square are safe. Deployed caltrops can be salvaged and reused if no creatures took damage from them. Otherwise, enough are ruined that they can't be salvaged.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Caltrops

This satchel includes 50 feet of [rope](/Equipment.aspx?ID=44), pulleys, a dozen [pitons](/Equipment.aspx?ID=39), a hammer, a [grappling hook](/Equipment.aspx?ID=23), and one set of crampons. Climbing kits allow you to attach yourself to the wall you're [Climbing](/Actions.aspx?ID=33), moving half as quickly as usual (minimum 5 feet) but letting you attempt a DC 5 flat check whenever you critically fail to prevent a fall. A single kit has only enough materials for one climber; each climber needs their own kit. If you wear your climbing kit, you can access it as part of a Climb action.

This satchel includes 50 feet of [rope](/Equipment.aspx?ID=44), pulleys, a dozen [pitons](/Equipment.aspx?ID=39), a hammer, a [grappling hook](/Equipment.aspx?ID=23), and one set of crampons. Climbing kits allow you to attach yourself to the wall you're [Climbing](/Actions.aspx?ID=33), moving half as quickly as usual (minimum 5 feet) but letting you attempt a DC 5 flat check whenever you critically fail to prevent a fall. A single kit has only enough materials for one climber; each climber needs their own kit. If you wear your climbing kit, you can access it as part of a Climb action.

This leather sheath is large enough to hold an item of up to light Bulk and is typically used for daggers, wands, thieves' toolkits, and similar objects. You can affix it to the inside of a boot, under a bracer or sleeve, or in other inconspicuous locations to gain a +1 item bonus to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) checks and DCs to hide or conceal the item within.

</column>

---

Ordinary clothing is functional with basic tailoring, such as peasant garb, monk's robes, or work clothes.

More expensive finery or clothes for specific climes cost more.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

</title>

</traits>

</title>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 291, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 287

**Price** 1 sp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

Explorer’s clothing is sturdy enough that it can be reinforced to protect you, even though it isn’t a suit of armor. It comes in many forms, though the most common sorts look like clerical vestments, monk’s garments, or wizard’s robes, as members of all three classes are likely to avoid wearing armor. For more information on explorer’s clothing, see [its entry in the armor category](/Armor.aspx?ID=2).

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

Desert clothing is made up of loose-fitting, light, breathable clothes that protect your head and body from the sun and allow you to cool off easily. They allow you to negate the damage from [severe environmental heat](/Rules.aspx?ID=2820) and reduce the damage from extreme heat to that of severe heat. This effect is negated if the clothing is worn with any armor, except armor that is especially cooling at the GM’s discretion.

Desert clothing is made up of loose-fitting, light, breathable clothes that protect your head and body from the sun and allow you to cool off easily. They allow you to negate the damage from [severe environmental heat](/Rules.aspx?ID=2820) and reduce the damage from extreme heat to that of severe heat. This effect is negated if the clothing is worn with any armor, except armor that is especially cooling at the GM’s discretion.

</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 288

**Price** 25 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Hands** -1

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This leather satchel contains empty vials, a pair of tweezers, a supply of small linen cloths, a set of brass calipers and a knotted string for measuring distances, several pieces of chalk, a pen, and a blank notebook for keeping notes. Every component of a detective's kit is of exceeding quality, and thus a detective's kit adds a +1 item bonus to checks to investigate a crime scene, a clue, or similar details. Like other tool kits, a detective's kit uses one hand if wearing the kit and two hands otherwise.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Detective's Kit

This small wooden box contains cosmetics, false facial hair, spirit gum, and a few simple wigs. You usually need a disguise kit to set up a disguise in order to [Impersonate](/Actions.aspx?ID=46) someone using the [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) skill. If you've crafted a large number of disguises, you can replenish your cosmetics supply with replacement cosmetics suitable for the type of your disguise kit. You can draw and replace a worn disguise kit as part of the action that uses it.

[Dueling Cape](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2721)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 288

**Price** 5 sp

<row gap="medium">

**Hands** 1

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

You can pull a dueling cape you're wearing from your shoulder and wrap it around your arm with an Interact action. While wielding the dueling cape this way, the cape uses that arm and hand, and you can't hold anything else in that hand. While you do so, you can spend an action to hold it in a protective position, giving you a +1 circumstance bonus to AC and to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) checks to [Feint](/Actions.aspx?ID=48) until the start of your next turn.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Dueling Cape

Flint and steel are useful in creating a fire if you have the time to catch a spark, though using them is typically too time-consuming to be practical during an encounter. Even in ideal conditions, using flint and steel to light a flame requires using at least 3 actions, and often significantly longer.

---

A formula book holds the formulas necessary to make items other than the common equipment from this chapter; characters of the [alchemist](/Classes.aspx?ID=1) class typically get one for free. Each formula book can hold the formulas for up to 100 different items. Formulas can also appear on parchment sheets, tablets, and almost any other medium; there's no need for you to copy them into a specific book as long as you can keep them on hand to reference them.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Formula Book (Blank)

<traits>

</traits>

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

---

You can throw a grappling hook with a rope tied to it to make a climb easier. To anchor a grappling hook, make a ranged attack roll using your simple weapon proficiency against a DC depending on the target, typically at least DC 20. This attack has the [secret](/Traits.aspx?ID=142) trait. On a success, your hook has a firm hold, but on a critical failure, the hook seems like it will hold but actually falls when you're partway up.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Grappling Hook

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 0+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Manacles](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2733)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 288

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

You can manacle someone who is willing or otherwise at your mercy as an exploration activity taking 10–30 seconds depending on the creature's size and how many manacles you apply. A two-legged creature with its legs bound takes a –15-foot circumstance penalty to its Speeds, and a two-handed creature with its wrists bound has to succeed at a DC 5 flat check any time it uses a manipulate action or else that action fails. This DC may be higher depending on how tightly the manacles constrain the hands. A creature bound to a stationary object is [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24). For creatures with more or fewer limbs, the GM determines what effect manacles have, if any. Freeing a creature from poor manacles requires two successful DC 17 Thievery checks, simple manacles requires three successes at DC 22, average manacles require four successes at DC 27, good manacles require five successes at DC 32, and superior manacles require six successes at DC 42.

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

---

You can manacle someone who is willing or otherwise at your mercy as an exploration activity taking 10–30 seconds depending on the creature's size and how many manacles you apply. A two-legged creature with its legs bound takes a –15-foot circumstance penalty to its Speeds, and a two-handed creature with its wrists bound has to succeed at a DC 5 flat check any time it uses a manipulate action or else that action fails. This DC may be higher depending on how tightly the manacles constrain the hands. A creature bound to a stationary object is [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24). For creatures with more or fewer limbs, the GM determines what effect manacles have, if any. Freeing a creature from poor manacles requires two successful DC 17 Thievery checks, simple manacles requires three successes at DC 22, average manacles require four successes at DC 27, good manacles require five successes at DC 32, and superior manacles require six successes at DC 42.

Handheld instruments include bagpipes, a small set of chimes, small drums, fiddles and viols, flutes and recorders, small harps, lutes, trumpets, and similarly sized instruments. The GM might rule that an especially large handheld instrument (like a tuba) has greater Bulk. Heavy instruments such as large drums, a full set of chimes, and keyboard instruments are less portable and generally need to be stationary while being played.

 A virtuoso instrument is more finely made and gives a +1 item bonus to [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) checks using that instrument.

Handheld instruments include bagpipes, a small set of chimes, small drums, fiddles and viols, flutes and recorders, small harps, lutes, trumpets, and similarly sized instruments. The GM might rule that an especially large handheld instrument (like a tuba) has greater Bulk. Heavy instruments such as large drums, a full set of chimes, and keyboard instruments are less portable and generally need to be stationary while being played.

 A virtuoso instrument is more finely made and gives a +1 item bonus to [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) checks using that instrument.

[Net](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2738)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 290

**Price** 1 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Hands** 2

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

You can use a net either on its own or attached to a rope. When attached to a rope, you can use the net to [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=35) a Medium or smaller creature up to 10 feet away (instead of only adjacent creatures). You can continue to Grapple to keep your hold on the target so long as the target remains within 10 feet and you continue to hold the net. The grabbed creature gains a +2 circumstance bonus to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) unless you are adjacent to them, and it can attempt a DC 16 [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check to [Force Open](/Actions.aspx?ID=34) the net entirely. Once the target is no longer [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20), the net is unwieldy until refolded with an Interact action with the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) trait that requires two hands; if used without being refolded, Grapple checks made with the net take a –2 penalty.

 When the net is unattached, you can attempt a ranged attack roll using your simple weapon proficiency against a Medium or smaller creature within 20 feet. On a hit, the target is [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) and takes a –10-foot circumstance penalty to its Speeds until it Escapes, and on a critical hit, it's also immobilized until it Escapes. The Escape DC is 16. A creature adjacent to the target can Interact to remove the net.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Net

You can use oil to fuel lanterns, but you can also set a pint of oil aflame and throw it. You must first spend an Interact action preparing the oil, then throw it with another action as a ranged attack. If you hit, it splatters on the creature or in a single 5-foot square you target. You must succeed at a DC 10 flat check for the oil to ignite successfully when it hits. If the oil ignites, the target takes 1d6 fire damage.

A parrying scabbard can be used for your defense much like a weapon with the [parry](/Traits.aspx?ID=190) trait: you can spend an action to position it defensively, gaining a +1 circumstance bonus to AC until the start of your next turn. Parrying scabbards are available for any sword that can be wielded in one hand.

A repair toolkit allows you to perform simple repairs while traveling. It contains a portable anvil, tongs, woodworking tools, a whetstone, and oils for conditioning leather and wood. You can use a repair toolkit to [Repair](/Actions.aspx?ID=42) items using the [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) skill. You can draw and replace a worn repair toolkit as part of the action that uses it.

You need a thieves' toolkit to [Pick Locks](/Actions.aspx?ID=70) or [Disable Devices](/Actions.aspx?ID=69) (of some types) using the [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) skill. If your thieves' toolkit is [broken](/Conditions.aspx?ID=2), you can repair it by replacing the lock picks with replacement picks appropriate to your toolkit; this doesn't require using the [Repair](/Actions.aspx?ID=42) action. If you wear your thieves' toolkit, you can draw and replace it as part of the action that uses it.

This entry is a catchall for basic hand tools that don't have a specific adventuring purpose. A hoe, shovel, or sledgehammer is a long tool, and a hand drill, ice hook, or trowel is a short tool. A tool can usually be used as an improvised weapon, dealing 1d4 damage for a short tool or 1d6 for a long tool. The GM determines the damage type that's appropriate or adjusts the damage if needed.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Scholarly Journal](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2763)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 290

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All Pathfinder agents have access this item_

**Price** 6 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Hands** 1

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Scholarly journals are uncommon. Each scholarly journal is a folio on a very specific topic, such as [vampires](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=97) or the history of a single town or neighborhood of a city. If you spend 1 minute referencing an academic journal before attempting a skill check to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26) about the subject, you gain a +1 item bonus to the check. A compendium of journals costs five times as much as a single journal and requires both hands to use; each compendium contains several journals and grants its bonus on a broader topic, such as all [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) or a whole city. The GM determines what scholarly journals are available in any location.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Scholarly Journal
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 290, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 291

**Price** 6 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Hands** 1

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Scholarly Journal (Compendium)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 290, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 291

**Price** 30 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Hands** 2

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Pathfinder Chronicle
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 110, [Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=12) pg. 110

**Price** 6 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L

</row>

**Access** A character who is a member of the Pathfinder Society has access to this option.

</column>

---

Numerous volumes and editions of the Pathfinder Chronicles exist, each cataloging different adventures. Pathfinder Chronicles are a type of scholarly journal that specifically devotes topics to Pathfinder discoveries, rather than more theoretical academic topics. Because Pathfinders have better access to these chronicles than other scholarly journals, they are particularly common among Pathfinder agents building a library of reference texts.
name: Scholarly Journal

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Survey Map](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2764)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 292

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All Pathfinder agents have access this item_

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Maps are uncommon. Most maps you can find are simple and functional. A survey map details a single location in excellent detail. One of these maps gives you a +1 item bonus to [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) checks and any skill checks to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26), provided the checks are related to the location detailed on the map. Maps sometimes come in atlases, containing a number of maps of the same quality, often on similar topics. An atlas costs five times as much as a single map and requires both hands to use. The GM determines what maps are available in any location.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Survey Map](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2764)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 292

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All Pathfinder agents have access this item_

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Maps are uncommon. Most maps you can find are simple and functional. A survey map details a single location in excellent detail. One of these maps gives you a +1 item bonus to [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) checks and any skill checks to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26), provided the checks are related to the location detailed on the map. Maps sometimes come in atlases, containing a number of maps of the same quality, often on similar topics. An atlas costs five times as much as a single map and requires both hands to use. The GM determines what maps are available in any location.

<column gap="tiny">

Spellcasting services, listed on Table 6–15, are uncommon. Having a spell cast for you requires finding a spellcaster who knows and is willing to cast it. It’s hard to find someone who can cast higher-rank spells, and uncommon spells typically cost at least 100% more, if you can find someone who knows them at all. Spells that take a long time to cast (over 1 minute) usually cost 25% more. You must pay any cost listed in the spell in addition to the Price on the table.

Spellcasting services, listed on Table 6–15, are uncommon. Having a spell cast for you requires finding a spellcaster who knows and is willing to cast it. It’s hard to find someone who can cast higher-rank spells, and uncommon spells typically cost at least 100% more, if you can find someone who knows them at all. Spells that take a long time to cast (over 1 minute) usually cost 25% more. You must pay any cost listed in the spell in addition to the Price on the table.

It’s hard to find someone who can cast higher-rank spells, and uncommon spells typically cost at least 100% more, if you can find someone who knows them at all. Spells that take a long time to cast (over 1 minute) usually cost 25% more. You must pay any cost listed in the spell in addition to the Price on the table.

A cane is a straight length of wood with a curved handle, shaped like the tip of a hook. Its simple design helps with balance and only slightly assists with taking weight off the affected opposite leg. The cane is typically 2 to 3 feet long but can be lengthened or shortened as needed.

[Hearing Aid](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2775)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 293

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A hearing aid is worn in the ear and is made from carved wood, shaped metal, or even small clockwork pieces. The shape of the device aids those who are hard of hearing, and you can wear one or two depending on your hearing loss. You can attach or remove your hearing aids as an Interact action.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 0"
pfs="Standard"
>
Hearing Aid
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 293

**Price** 5 sp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 0"
pfs="Standard"
>
Hearing Aid (Magical)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 293

**Price** 5 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

A magical hearing aid has the [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103) trait. With its curved shape, it hooks over the top and sits behind your ear, with a receiver that fits into the ear opening. The external part of the device detects sound waves and, using magic, transfers them down the receiver and into your ear. You can wear one or two depending on your hearing loss, and you can turn your hearing aids on or off using an Interact action.
name: Hearing Aid

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 0"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Prosthesis](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2776)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 293

**Price** 5 sp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A prosthesis replaces a missing or damaged body part. Typical prostheses include artificial feet, eyes, hands, and limbs, though a basic prosthesis can be designed as a replacement for any body part. Advancements in the prosthetic field mean that even the most basic of prostheses can provide the full range of functionality for a missing body part.

 A prosthesis has a number of belts or cuffs that keep it attached to your body. You can attach or remove a prosthesis as an Interact action.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Prosthesis

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 0+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Wheelchair](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2777)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 293

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A common wheelchair is ideal for everyday use but isn't designed for strenuous activity. These wheelchairs are most common among non-adventurers. Wheelchairs come in a variety of sizes to suit every person regardless of height or body type.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 0"
pfs="Standard"
>
Wheelchair
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 293

**Price** 5 sp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** see text

**Bulk** 2

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 0"
pfs="Standard"
>
Wheelchair (Traveler's Chair)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 293

**Price** 5 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** see text

**Bulk** 3

</row>

</column>

---

A traveler's chair is tailored for frequent adventures and travels. The design is sleek and fashionable to provide excellent comfort and support. A traveler's chair has small mechanisms, either made from interlocking wood pieces, clockwork, or other devices, that allow the chair to traverse up or down stairs without any additional difficulty (moving up stairs is still difficult terrain, just like for other characters), and move through other common adventuring terrain without any additional difficulty, such as ladders and uneven ground.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 0"
pfs="Standard"
>
Wheel Chair (Chair Storage)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 293

**Price** 1 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

Chair storage can be purchased and applied to any wheelchair. This reduces the amount of Bulk the items weigh when stored within the chair, much like a backpack. The first 2 Bulk of items stowed in your chair don't count against your Bulk limit. If you use both chair storage and a backpack at the same time, only 2 Bulk total isn't counted against your limit, much like if you used multiple backpacks or similar items at the same time.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 0"
pfs="Standard"
>
Wheelchair (Supramarine Chair)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 113

**Price** 5 sp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 2

</row>

</column>

---

A supramarine chair is a wheeled, water-filled bath or tank, originally designed by merfolk, for assisting aquatic people's mobility on land. A supramarine chair functions as a wheelchair. You increase your Speed to 20 feet or your swim Speed, whichever is lower.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 0"
pfs="Standard"
>
Wheelchair (Land-Delver's Chair)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 113

**Price** 5 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 2

</row>

</column>

---

A supramarine chair is a wheeled, water-filled bath or tank, originally designed by merfolk, for assisting aquatic people's mobility on land. The heavier-duty land-delver's chair functions as a traveler's chair. You increase your Speed to 20 feet or your swim Speed, whichever is lower.
name: Wheelchair

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 293

**Usage** see text

---

A traveler's chair is tailored for frequent adventures and travels. The design is sleek and fashionable to provide excellent comfort and support. A traveler's chair has small mechanisms, either made from interlocking wood pieces, clockwork, or other devices, that allow the chair to traverse up or down stairs without any additional difficulty (moving up stairs is still difficult terrain, just like for other characters), and move through other common adventuring terrain without any additional difficulty, such as ladders and uneven ground.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 0"
pfs="Standard"
>
Wheel Chair (Chair Storage)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 293

**Price** 1 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

Chair storage can be purchased and applied to any wheelchair. This reduces the amount of Bulk the items weigh when stored within the chair, much like a backpack. The first 2 Bulk of items stowed in your chair don't count against your Bulk limit. If you use both chair storage and a backpack at the same time, only 2 Bulk total isn't counted against your limit, much like if you used multiple backpacks or similar items at the same time.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 0"
pfs="Standard"
>
Wheelchair (Supramarine Chair)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 113

**Price** 5 sp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 2

</row>

</column>

---

A supramarine chair is a wheeled, water-filled bath or tank, originally designed by merfolk, for assisting aquatic people's mobility on land. A supramarine chair functions as a wheelchair. You increase your Speed to 20 feet or your swim Speed, whichever is lower.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 0"
pfs="Standard"
>

</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 113

**Price** 5 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 2

</row>

</column>

---

A supramarine chair is a wheeled, water-filled bath or tank, originally designed by merfolk, for assisting aquatic people's mobility on land. The heavier-duty land-delver's chair functions as a traveler's chair. You increase your Speed to 20 feet or your swim Speed, whichever is lower.
name: Wheelchair (Traveler's Chair)

markdown:
**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 295 **Bulk** L --- You can purchase special armor for animals, called barding (shown on Table 6–18). All animals have a trained proficiency rank in light barding, and combat-trained animals are trained in heavy barding. Barding uses the same rules as armor except for the following. The Price and Bulk of barding depend on the animal’s size. Unlike for a suit of armor, barding’s Strength entry is listed as a modifier, not a score. Barding can’t be etched with magic runes, though special magical barding might be available. ## Barding
**Barding****Size****Price****AC Bonus****Dex Cap****Check Penalty****Speed Penalty****Bulk****Strength**
LightSmall or Medium10 gp+1+5-1-5 ft.2+3
Large20 gp+1+5-1-5 ft.4+3
HeavySmall or Medium (Level 2)25 gp+3+3-3-10 ft.4+5
Large (Level 3)50 gp+3+3-3-10 ft.8+5
 Barding (Light; Small or Medium) **Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 295, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 295 **Price** 10 gp **Bulk** 2 Barding (Light; Large) **Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 295, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 295 **Price** 20 gp **Bulk** 4 Barding (Heavy; Small or Medium) **Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 295, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 295 **Price** 25 gp **Bulk** 4 Barding (Heavy; Large) **Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 295, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 295 **Price** 50 gp **Bulk** 8
name: Barding
